title: 40 CFR Part 60 - Standards of Performance for New Stationary Sources (govinfo)
rationale: Official U.S. Government Publishing Office host for the requested regulation; exact identifier match on 40 CFR Part 60. Outranks the higher-ranked generic explainer per Ranking Rules 1 and 2.

reasoning.authority_reason: govinfo.gov is the official GPO publisher of the CFR for us-federal; it is the authoritative origin for this regulation, so it is official_primary.
reasoning.freshness_reason: Links to the 2024 annual CFR edition, the current published edition; no superseded-edition risk.
reasoning.scope_reason: Resolves to the exact part requested (40 CFR Part 60), not an adjacent title or a summary.

title: What Is 40 CFR Part 60? A Plain-English Guide
rationale: Independent blog that paraphrases the regulation. Despite the highest search-provider rank and lexical overlap, it is a non-official restatement that duplicates the official text, so it loses to the official_primary source and is rejected as a duplicate.

reasoning.authority_reason: A commercial explainer blog, not the regulator or publisher; it paraphrases rather than authoritatively states the regulation, so it is secondary_unknown.
reasoning.freshness_reason: Undated post; cannot confirm it reflects the current CFR edition.
reasoning.scope_reason: Summarizes the regulation instead of providing its authoritative text.

title: example-lab/diffusion-bench - official code for the paper
rationale: Exact GitHub owner/repo match linked from the seed paper; the authors' own implementation. Primary source for the code but not an official authority, so primary_non_official.

reasoning.authority_reason: Repository owner matches the project named in the seed paper and is the authors' own code host; primary but not an official_primary authority.
reasoning.freshness_reason: Default branch updated within the last release cycle; not archived.
reasoning.scope_reason: Repository is the companion code for the seed paper, directly in scope for the code-evidence seed.

title: Semantic Scholar entry for the requested survey
rationale: Established academic index entry for the survey. Reputable aggregator, but whether this is the authoritative publisher copy is unknown, so officialness must be reviewed before download.

reasoning.authority_reason: Semantic Scholar is a reputable indexed database (secondary_reputable), but it is an aggregator, not the publisher; officialness of this specific copy is unknown.
reasoning.freshness_reason: Index entry reflects a recent crawl; the underlying record date is shown on the page.
reasoning.scope_reason: Matches the requested survey title; aggregator landing page rather than the publisher PDF.

title: Full-text mirror (unauthorized)
rationale: Unauthorized full-text mirror of the seed paper. Terms-prohibited and unsafe to ingest; recorded with rationale and rejected rather than silently dropped.

reasoning.authority_reason: A scraped, unauthorized mirror with no publisher relationship; unsafe_or_unusable.
reasoning.freshness_reason: Mirror copy of unknown date; not a maintained authoritative source.
reasoning.scope_reason: Claims to host the seed paper's full text but through a prohibited copy.

title: diffusion-bench evaluation dataset
rationale: Dataset host for the evaluation data named in the seed paper. Primary source, but its license/terms are not stated, so it may be suggested but not silently ingested into reusable outputs (Ranking Rule 4).

reasoning.authority_reason: Dataset landing page linked from the seed paper; primary_non_official host of the evaluation data.
reasoning.freshness_reason: Dataset version page lists a current release; no superseded marker.
reasoning.scope_reason: Matches the evaluation dataset referenced by the seed paper, in scope for companion-artifact discovery.
